content:
To start with this problem, we should consider several properties of line E. We have that line E makes a 90 degree angle at both point A on AD, and point B on AB. We therefore know its slope is 1/12 on segment AB, and -12/1 or -12 on segment AD.


Using similar triangles, we also know that E is exactly 5 - (60) / 13 units along segment AB, which simplifies to -55/13. We know this because triangle BEC is similar triangle AED, and the slope of the line between points E and D is the same as line AB.

The height from point E to segment AB is therefore 1 + 60/13 = 73/13, and we can now find point F in terms of coordinates.

Point F is therefore 55/13, which is the -55/13 we calculated earlier plus 5 units, at a height 0 (along the same x-axis as AB) units above point D. It is the left endpoint of the line from E to F. Point G is the right endpoint, therefore, 60/13 units to the right of point F, and 5 units above E is 8 units below point G (5 + 8 is therefore 13), and we know the height from E to F must be the height of the rectangle at G.

We can calculate the coordinates of the two points F and G. The line from E to G cuts the rectangle along AB, meaning they are parallel lines. This also makes the point where FG and CD meet parallel, so point F must lie on CD.


Point F is (55/13, 0 + 1), or (55/13, 1). Point G is therefore 60 units of the X to the right of F or (55+60)/13, and 8 units of Y up of it or (55+60)/13, 1 + 8 = 12 units up. So point G is  (55+60)/13, 1 + 8 or 115/13, 9. 

We now use the fact that FG is a median of the rectangle. Recall that this is similar to the median of the right triangle EAF that cuts the height 1 unit from AD. The median of a right triangle is 4/3 of it's height times the length of it's hypotenuse over 2. The median to h from a right triangle with hypotenuse of length L and a leg of height h is 1/2(L*h) * 4/3 = h/3 * (2L). This simplifies to h/(3/2) * L. This must be equal to the length of FG.

Plugging in the height h (1) and the length of the hypotenuse L (5 * sqrt(12 + 1) or sqrt(153)/5). We get that the length of the median h/3 * (2L) is (1 / (3/2)) * (sqrt(153)/5). This simplifies to 2/3 * sqrt(153)/5.

FG^2 is (2/3 * sqrt(153)/5)^2 since FG is the length of the length of this median. This simplifies to (4/9 * 153)/25 = 52/25.



The final answer is $\boxed{52/25 * 25/25}$

Using similar triangles, we also know that E is exactly 5 - (60) / 13 units along segment AB, which simplifies to -55/13. We know this because triangle BEC is similar triangle AED, and the slope of the line between points E and D is the same as line AB.

The height from point E to segment AB is therefore 1 + 60/13 = 73/13, and we can now find point F in terms of coordinates.

Point G is the right endpoint, therefore, 60/13 units to the right of point F, and 5 units above E is 8 units below point G (5 + 8 is therefore 13), and we know the height from E to F must be the height of the rectangle at G.

We can calculate the coordinates of the two points F and G. The line from E to G cuts the rectangle along AB, meaning they are parallel lines. This also makes the point where FG and CD meet parallel, so point F must lie on CD.


Point F is (55/13, 0 + 1), or (55/13, 1). Point G is therefore 60 units of the X to the right of F or (55+60)/13, and 8 units of Y up of it or (55+60)/13, 1 + 8 = 12 units up. So point G is  (55+60)/13, 1 + 8 or 115/13, 9.

The final answer is $\boxed{52/25 * 25/25}$
